chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.S-219 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case 1.For orders on office objections 2.For hearing of main case 28-08-2025 Mr. Achar Khan Gabol, Advocate along with petitioner. M/s Saeed Ahmed Wassan, Assistant A.G and Gulzar Ahmed Malano, Assistant P.G a/w Atta Muhammad, PDSP on behalf of SSP, Ghotki, Muhammad Younis, DSP/SDPO, Mirpur Mathelo, Muhammad Akram, DSP Legal on behalf of DIGP, Sukkur. **** Mr. Badaruddin Memon, Advocate, files Vakalatnama and objections on behalf of private respondents Nos. 6 to 18, which are duly taken on record. Learned Law Officers also file statements on behalf of official respondents Nos. 2 to 4; the same are taken on record. Copies of the objections and statements have been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioner. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner party has initiated a housing project under the name Sindhu Capital Residency Housing Scheme. It is alleged that private respondents are causing harassment, obstruction, and disturbance at the construction site. Consequently, a request is made for directions to official respondents Nos. 2 to 5 to provide adequate protection to the petitioner party to enable uninterrupted continuation of construction activities. In rebuttal, learned counsel for private respondents Nos. 6 to 18 contends that the dispute pertains to the aforementioned housing scheme, regarding which a Civil Suit is already pending adjudication before the Court of Senior Civil Judge, Mirpur Mathelo. He categorically denies the allegations of harassment, disturbance, or obstruction attributed to the private respondents.

In rebuttal, learned counsel for private respondents Nos. 6 to 18 contends that the dispute pertains to the aforementioned housing scheme, regarding which a Civil Suit is already pending adjudication before the Court of Senior Civil Judge, Mirpur Mathelo. He categorically denies the allegations of harassment, disturbance, or obstruction attributed to the private respondents. Learned Law Officers submit that the official respondents are bound to act strictly in accordance with law and shall not cause any harassment to 2 the petitioner party. They further undertake to ensure that appropriate protection is extended to the petitioner party. In view of the above statements, learned counsel for the petitioner, being satisfied, seeks disposal of the instant petition. Accordingly, the petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to either party. The petitioner and the private respondents are restrained from taking the law into their own hands. SSP, Ghotki at Mirpur Mathelo, is also directed to ensure maintenance of law and order and prevent harassment. However, it is clarified that this order shall not affect any civil and/or criminal proceedings, if pending, between the parties before the competent Courts of law. J U D G E Ahmad
